questions.question_text: A wild Promissory Note appears! On Jan 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp entered into a 5-year lease. The present value of lease payments is $50,000. Useful life is 7 years. No purchase option. How do you classify this encounter under ASC 842?

explanations.Operating Lease: A direct hit! 🎯 Because the lease term (5 years) is 71% of the economic life (less than the 75% threshold), and no other criteria are met, it is an operating lease.
explanations.Finance Lease: A miscast! 🌩️ A finance lease requires meeting at least one of the OWL-NES criteria. None are met here.
explanations.Short-term Lease: Not quite! 🧟‍♂️ A short-term lease must have a maximum possible term of 12 months or less.
explanations.Sales-type Lease: Wrong spellbook! 📖 Sales-type leases are classifications used by the LESSOR, not the lessee.

questions.question_text: A corporation issues 10-year bonds with a face value of $100,000 at a discount. Which spell correctly describes the effect of amortizing this discount over time using the effective interest method?

explanations.Increases Interest Expense each period: Excellent cast! 🪄 Amortizing a bond discount increases the carrying value of the bond. Since Interest Expense = Carrying Value × Effective Rate, a rising carrying value means interest expense increases.
explanations.Decreases Interest Expense each period: A fizzle! 💨 This describes the amortization of a bond PREMIUM, which reduces the carrying value over time.
explanations.Increases Cash Paid each period: Careful! 🛑 The cash paid for interest is fixed. It is calculated as Face Value × Stated Coupon Rate.
explanations.Decreases Cash Paid each period: Careful! 🛑 The cash paid for interest is fixed based on the contract terms and does not change.

questions.question_text: The Guild Master demands an inventory valuation! Read the attached scrolls (exhibits) and calculate the ending inventory balance using the FIFO method.

exhibits.title: Scroll of Beginning Inventory
exhibits.content: January 1: 100 potions at 10 gold pieces each.

exhibits.title: Ledger of Purchases
exhibits.content:
March 15: 200 potions at 12 gold pieces each. 
October 10: 100 potions at 15 gold pieces each.

exhibits.title: Ledger of Sales
exhibits.content: Total potions sold during the year: 250.

tasks.prompt: Enter the Total Ending Inventory Value (in gold pieces):

explanations.correct: Masterful calculation! 🧙‍♂️ Total available was 400 potions. You sold 250, leaving 150 in ending inventory. Under FIFO, the ending inventory consists of the NEWEST costs: 100 potions @ 15g (1500g) + 50 potions @ 12g (600g) = 2100 gold pieces.
explanations.incorrect: You triggered a trap! 🕸️ Remember, under FIFO (First-In, First-Out), the oldest costs are sold first. Your ending inventory of 150 potions should consist of the most recent October purchases, and then dip into the March purchases.

questions.question_text: A temporal rift opens! Evaluate the following event that occurred on February 15, Year 2, before the Year 1 financial scrolls were issued. Determine the correct accounting treatment for the Year 1 financial statements.

exhibits.title: Courier's Report
exhibits.content: On February 15, Year 2, a major warehouse containing 20% of the company's inventory was destroyed by a rogue dragon fire. The inventory was uninsured.

tasks.prompt: Type 'Recognize' if you must adjust the Year 1 statements, or 'Disclose' if you only add a footnote:

explanations.correct: Time manipulation successful! ⏳ The fire occurred in Year 2. Because the condition did not exist at the balance sheet date (Dec 31, Year 1), it is a nonrecognized subsequent event requiring footnote disclosure only.
explanations.incorrect: A timeline paradox! 🌀 You only 'Recognize' (adjust the numbers) if the underlying condition existed at the balance sheet date. Dragon fires are sudden and unpredictable.

questions.question_text: You enter the Citadel of Government Accounting. Under the modified accrual basis of accounting, when should revenue be recognized by a governmental fund?
options: When it is earned, regardless of cash flow
When it is measurable and available
When the budget is officially adopted
questions.correct_answer: When it is measurable and available

explanations.When cash is received: Too simple! 🧟‍♂️ That is the pure cash basis of accounting, which GAAP does not permit for governmental funds.
explanations.When it is earned, regardless of cash fl: Wrong realm! 🌌 That describes full accrual accounting, which is used for government-wide statements and proprietary funds, but not governmental funds.
explanations.When it is measurable and available: Wisdom prevails! 🦉 Governmental funds use modified accrual, meaning revenues are recognized when they are 'measurable' and 'available' to finance expenditures of the current period.
explanations.When the budget is officially adopted: A misstep! 🛡️ Adopting the budget triggers a budgetary journal entry (Estimated Revenues), but actual revenue is recognized later.

questions.question_text: An alchemist sells a potion and a 1-year guarantee as a bundled package for 100 gold pieces. The potion normally sells standalone for 80 gold, and the guarantee for 40 gold. Under ASC 606, how much revenue is allocated to the potion?

explanations.100 gold pieces: An illusion! 🌫️ You cannot allocate the entire transaction price to just one performance obligation when two distinct goods/services are provided.
explanations.80 gold pieces: A trap! 🪤 You cannot just use the standalone price if the bundle is sold at a discount. You must allocate the discount proportionally.
explanations.66.67 gold pieces: Alchemical mastery! 🧪 Total standalone = 120 (80+40). Potion ratio = 80/120 (or 2/3). Total price 100 × 2/3 = 66.67 gold pieces.
explanations.50 gold pieces: Incorrect division! ⚔️ You cannot split the revenue 50/50 unless their standalone selling prices are exactly equal.

questions.question_text: Company A acquires 100% of Company B for $500,000. Company B's net assets have a book value of $400,000 and a fair value of $450,000. What is the Goodwill recognized from this conquest?

explanations.$100,000: A rookie mistake! 🛡️ This is the difference between the purchase price and the BOOK value. Goodwill must be calculated using FAIR value.
explanations.$50,000: You've tamed the dragon! 🐉 Goodwill is the excess of the purchase price over the FAIR VALUE of the identifiable net assets. $500,000 purchase price - $450,000 fair value = $50,000 Goodwill.
explanations.$0: A fizzle! 💨 If the purchase price was equal to or less than the fair value of net assets, Goodwill would be $0 (or a bargain purchase gain), but here you paid a premium.
explanations.$450,000: Way off target! 🏹 This is the fair value of the net assets acquired, not the residual Goodwill calculation.

questions.question_text: The Guild is facing a lawsuit! Read the counsel's parchment and determine the correct liability to accrue on the balance sheet.

exhibits.title: Letter from Legal Counsel
exhibits.content: It is 'probable' that the Guild will lose this lawsuit. We estimate the damages will be somewhere between 100,000 and 200,000 gold pieces. No single amount in that range is a better estimate than any other.

tasks.prompt: Enter the amount to accrue as a liability (enter 0 if only a disclosure is required):

explanations.correct: Justice is served! ⚖️ Under US GAAP, if a loss is probable and estimable within a range, and no amount is better than another, you accrue the MINIMUM amount in the range (100,000) and disclose the rest.
explanations.incorrect: You've been held in contempt! 🔨 If it is probable and a range is given with no best estimate, US GAAP requires accruing the MINIMUM amount in the range. (Note: IFRS requires the midpoint).

questions.question_text: A wizarding tech company spends $50,000 on routine Research & Development to create a new spellcasting device. How should this cost be accounted for under US GAAP?

explanations.Capitalized as an Intangible Asset: An illegal charm! 🚫 Unlike IFRS (which allows capitalization of development costs if certain criteria are met), US GAAP generally prohibits this.
explanations.Expensed immediately: Swift casting! ⚡ Under US GAAP, virtually all routine Research and Development (R&D) costs must be expensed as incurred due to the uncertainty of future economic benefits.
explanations.Capitalized as Inventory: A critical failure! 🎲 R&D costs are not capitalized as inventory because the product has not yet reached technological feasibility or production.
explanations.Amortized over 5 years: A misfire! 🔥 You cannot amortize a cost that is not capitalized in the first place.

questions.question_text: A wealthy merchant donates 1,000,000 gold pieces to a hospital, stipulating the funds must be held in perpetuity, but the investment income can be used for operations. How does the hospital classify this upon receipt?

explanations.Net Assets Without Donor Restrictions: A violation of trust! 🧟‍♂️ Because the donor explicitly stated the principal must be held in perpetuity, it comes with a heavy restriction.
explanations.Net Assets With Donor Restrictions: A blessed donation! 🙏 Because the principal must be maintained in perpetuity (an endowment), it increases Net Assets With Donor Restrictions.
explanations.Deferred Revenue Liability: A misunderstanding! 💡 Contributions are recognized as revenue when received, not deferred, even if they have restrictions (unless they are conditional).
explanations.Other Financing Sources: Wrong realm! 🌌 'Other Financing Sources' is a term used in governmental fund accounting, not Not-For-Profit accounting.
